title: Cirrus: Workaround artifact upload/download race
body:
See also: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14385
Docs ref: https://cirrus-ci.org/guide/writing-tasks/#manual-cache-upload

For some reason unknown at this time, internal to Cirrus-CI is a race
condition.  Regardless of the cause, the result is downloading artifacts
by dependent tasks isn't always reliable.  Under some conditions, a 404
error is returned from the Cirrus API endpoint despite the repo.tbz
artifact existing in the backend GCS bucket.

Cirrus support indicated that their logic behind artifact upload and
download is fairly complex.  They suggested the cirrus agent (process
responsible for executing commands on VMs and containers) publishes the
location of a simple REST cache interface.  This commit implements this
recommendation.

Note: Since cache cannot be shared across VM and container-based tasks,
convert the `consistency` task to run on a VM.  Also update the
`docker-py` task's `TEST_ENVIRON` variable, since it was already
converted a long time ago.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

Ouch, sorry, this is too big for me to review right now (past end of day) and I'm unavailable tomorrow & Saturday. If this needs to go in soon, well, just merge it and see how the weekend goes.

> Ouch, sorry, this is too big for me to review right now (past end of day) and I'm unavailable tomorrow & Saturday. If this needs to go in soon, well, just merge it and see how the weekend goes.

Thanks @edsantiago I understand, it's no problem this has been broken for over a week, a few more days won't hurt.
Force-pushed with @fkorotkov recommendation + finished implementing updates to `artifacts` task.

force-pushed: Removed `[CI:BUILD]`  and rebased against `main`.

(leaving this as a 'draft' since I only *think* it will work.  If CI passes, I'll finalize it for review).
force-push: minor comment fix.
force-push: Fixed bug with quoted output redirect, and rebased.

> Also, CI is still broken

This is interesting...there's no reason I can see why [that task should have given a 404](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4984819804274688).  The [`swagger task` started at "roughly" the same time](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6115882396024832), hit the same URL, and got the tarball just fine :confused:
I'm going to press "re-run" on the `Test Code Consistency` task...

...still fails.  Ahh, this may be significant: The `Test Code Consistency` task runs in a container not a VM.

@fkorotkov ping - I vaguely remember seeing this before.  Is there any way to allow (or a workaround) for container tasks to access agent HTTP cache originally posted by a VM?

Example, both tasks depend on 'build' (a VM task):
* [VM Task](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6115882396024832)
* [Container Task](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4984819804274688)

Oh. The issue is that the VM one is using your GCP project to store caches and the failing one uses managed by us `container` instance and tries to read the cache key in our GCP project. Cirrus is storing all the logs/caches/artifacts using credentials associated with a task. So for your tasks everything is stored in your GCP project and the `container` one in our project.

This observation helped me to identify a potential issue which caused the initial 404s with `artifacts`. I'm implementing a fix will update you here once it's deployed. So maybe you don't even need this change.

> So maybe you don't even need this change.

Oh! That would be great!
So if we do need to use this PR, my backup plan is to just convert this one problematic task into a VM.  I checked, and it's really the only one having a problem accessing cache.
My improvement just got deployed so hopefully now you won't see the random 404s with the artifacts API.

> My improvement just got deployed so hopefully now you won't see the random 404s with the artifacts API.

Thanks for the update, I'll poke some re-run buttons and see.  If not, we've got a daily job for `main` that will run today and send me an e-mail if it breaks.

@fkorotkov I re-ran the necessary `build` parent task (uploads cache) and a previously failing downstream `swagger` (child) task. Curiously, [the first run caught a 404](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5584858410909696) but [the next one worked](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6156239385133056) (I think Cirrus did an auto-retry)!  There was maybe 10 seconds between the two if that matters.  In any case, it seems there may still be some kind of race condition here: Child (dependent) tasks are allowed to start executing prior to parent artifacts being **accessible**.  Also, I'm still perplexed how/why this problem only ever seems to appear on branches and never PRs :confused: 

Note: I'm sensitive that fixing this perfectly may be impractical for you.  If that's the case, I would propose a documentation update to warn other users not to rely on artifact-based passing between tasks, and use the cache mechanism instead.

Something very weird was going on. From Cirrus CI standpoint I see two GET requests to `/v1/artifact/build/6547728472735744/Build%20for%20fedora-36/repo/repo.tbz` that succeded at `11:52:06.074 EDT` and `11:53:06.932 EDT`. Before that it 404ed at `10:56:14.303 EDT`. The `11:52:06` matches the [tasks that failed for you](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5584858410909696): it was created at `11:51:14` and it took 11 seconds to initiate VM boot and additional 31 seconds to boot the VM.

That led me to thinking about our CDN... And it appeared Google Cloud CDN caches 404s for 120 seconds by default. 🤦 I've fixed it. Thank you for your patience and sorry for your inconveniences!

>And it appeared Google Cloud CDN caches 404s for 120 seconds by default.

Oh wow!  I'm amazed you were able to track that down :scream: 

> I've fixed it. Thank you for your patience and sorry for your inconveniences!

That's great news.  And thank you so much for seeing the problem through.  I'm still amazed it was a problem in the CDN.

Okay I'll keep an eye on the master branch (nothings merged in the last few hours) and close this PR when we're confirmed "out of the woods".

Thanks again.
Being impatient and excited: I re-ran the most recent failed build, [swagger's clone snagged the artifact right away!](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6509383642251264?logs=clone#L0) :clap:

@fkorotkov I'm still seeing 404's, [for example in this build](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5880893964288000).  It's up to you, but I think maybe this isn't worth fixing, other than maybe in the docs.  You pointed out early on that the artifact API is much more complex than the agent-cache.

I'm "okay" with going ahead with this workaround PR, even if it means taking another task into a VM from a container (we're use to it by now).  We've given it our best, and sometimes it just needs to be okay with moving on to other things.  I still appreciate all the work/help you've provided, that doesn't change :smiley:
Force-push: Rebased on `main` and enhanced the `build for *` task comment to indicate only VMs can use its cache.
Force-push: Fix for `artifacts` task (was running in a container).

Oh. The issue with this failure is because the validation step was skipped so the unit tests started right away. `depends_on` logic only looks one level. Let me see how easy it is to fix on our side...

I've made a PR to fix the dependency resolution by checking a transitive set of `depends_on` for a given task. But it's Friday and we don't deploy changes touching critical paths on Fridays. I'll deploy it on Sunday night or on Monday.

> But it's Friday and we don't deploy changes touching critical paths on Fridays

No, this is not urgent for us because we have a workaround and the problem has been going on a long while.  Stability and reliability is much more important for us, so please take whatever time is needed.  

I think we're lucky to hit this problem in podman, since it's so high up our stack.  If this problem happened on a podman dependency, that would be much worse.  So we have time on our side here.

I'm happy to have this workaround merge, since you said it's a simpler mechanism anyway *AND* at some future point you fix the dependency issue.  We can do both :)
I think this is ready to merge (unless I'm missing something?).
This is enormous and scary and risky; I was hoping that @fkorotkov would merge those changes on the Cirrus end, and we could forget about this PR.

> This is enormous and scary and risky

Edit: This change adds 16 lines net, many of which are comments.

Perhaps this will alleviate some concerns:

* Using cache is how we _use_ to do this as of few months ago, it worked mostly well for a very long time
   but the YAML was kind of messy and there were some breakdowns (3 or so, IIRC).
* I switched to using artifacts, mistakenly (hindsight) believing it was simpler because the YAML was a bit easier to read.
* When artifacts broke in strange ways recently, some of Fedor's first words were "The API for artifacts is complex" and "cache is much simpler".
* This PR demonstrates the switch to HTTP-caching works (at least in this context) - More testing could be done here to increase confidence.

I'm willing to wait and see if `main` clears up, but I'd still prefer this simpler agent-cache solution.  I don't think we need to re-learn the lesson of "complex things are inherently more failure-prone" :grin:
I s'pose the reverse is also valid: If main clears up, close this and re-open later if/when artifacts break or race.
Just an update from our side: fix on our side was deployed around an hour ago. So skipped tasks won't preemptively start it dependents.
Thanks for the update @fkorotkov I'll keep an eye on the `main` branch today.

in fact...[here's the first `main` build that passed all on it's own.](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/4636333237862400).  Two more are running at the moment, I'll keep an eye out for them to go green.

Thanks again @fkorotkov

Leaving this open for when @edsantiago gets back.  I guess I'm okay either way (merge or close).  My main motivation/leaning toward "merge" relates to relying on simple mechanisms over more complex, assuming that will better maintain long-term reliable operations.
force-push: rebased (conflict in `.cirrus.yml`)

ugh, oh noes!  @fkorotkov maybe this is my fault:  I noticed when my #14438 merged[*] a few hours ago, [we started getting 404's again](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5445424109584384) :cry: The good news is, I smashed the re-run button and it seems to be working okay now.  So maybe it's related to the CDN negative-caching?

[*] My change was simply replacing all our `skip`s with `only_if`s, but I could see that maybe screwing up the dependency chasing logic

@fkorotkov ahh no, I think I see the problem.  It's the dependency chasing, it's stopping when a task isn't created due to `only_if`...that kind of makes sense.  I'll just go through and add a direct `build` dependency where needed.  Sorry for the alarm.

Fix: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14521
Force-push: Rebased on https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14521

Ed's gone next week, and clearly didn't get to review this, which is okay.  In the interest of reducing the number of "meh" PRs, I'm going to close this.  We can re-open or do something similar later if the API complexity of artifact-passing proves problematic.
